text.title: Django Code Placement Decision Rules
text.summary: The placement tree and layer responsibility table for Django applications: where every type of code belongs.

rule.statement: Code that changes the database (CREATE/UPDATE/DELETE) belongs in services/. Code that makes external API calls belongs in services/ or integrations/. Pure functions and transformations (no side effects, no DB) belong in utils/. HTTP request/response handling belongs in views/. Data structure definitions belong in models/. Input validation belongs in serializers/. Background/async jobs belong in tasks/.
rule.rationale: This partition ensures every layer is independently testable: utils/ has no dependencies to mock, services/ has no HTTP context to set up, views/ has no business logic to test exhaustively.

rule.statement: The dependency direction is strict: views/ depends on services/ and serializers/. Services/ depends on models/ and utils/. Utils/ depends on nothing (or stdlib only). Never reverse this direction.
rule.rationale: Reversing the dependency direction (services importing views, utils importing services) creates circular imports and makes the layers impossible to test in isolation.

list.item: views/ — HTTP handling, request validation, response formatting. Depends on: services, serializers.
services/ — Business logic, DB writes, external API calls. Depends on: models, utils.
utils/ — Pure functions, helpers, transformations. Depends on: nothing or stdlib.
models/ — Data structure, DB schema, ORM metadata. Depends on: Django ORM.
serializers/ — Input validation, output formatting. Depends on: models.
tasks/ — Background jobs, async processing. Depends on: services.
integrations/ — Third-party API wrappers. Depends on: utils.
